label: 654_external validity_experiments_validity_experimental
description: Economists use both field experiments and laboratory experiments to measure causal effects. Field experiments offer more natural settings but less control compared to lab experiments, which can be highly controlled but may lack realism. The choice between the two depends on the research question.
